purpose: Compact decision map for narrowing and clarifying the /work index without redesigning the site. Use this before reading deeper runtime files.

referenceBias.notes: Use calm hierarchy and buyer-readable proof. Do not turn /work into a creative-studio archive or an enterprise dashboard.

pageRole.job: Proof of capability — curated client work with measurable outcomes.

keep: Flagship work as the main entry point.
Supporting case studies as a secondary browsing layer.
Connected-system strips under parent engagements instead of flattening all child builds into one tier.
Restrained studio support lower on the page.
Existing accessibility patterns for tabs/segmented navigation where they remain useful.
reduceOrRemove: Signal-strip or status-strip UI that makes the page feel operational instead of editorial.
A second filter row or any parallel control layer competing with the case studies.
Dense card footers, extra chrome, and dashboard-like metadata stacks.
Equal visual weight between flagship parent studies and system-child projects.
Anything that makes a visitor work to understand which proof matters most.
recommendedSectionOrder: Connected system strips under parent studies

flagship.countBias: 4-5 max before supporting proof takes over
flagship.notes: These are the trust anchors. They should be easier to notice than every other tier on the page.

supporting.notes: Compact and skimmable. Do not let this tier overpower flagship proof.

systemChildren.displayRule: Always subordinate to the parent engagement they came from.
systemChildren.notes: Useful for range and mechanism clarity, but they should read like inside-the-system proof rather than equal flagship entries.

browsingRule.preferredBehavior: Keep or simplify to a single segmented navigation model. Do not add new filters in this pass.
browsingRule.labelBias: Prefer buyer-readable or proof-readable labels over internal taxonomy jargon.
browsingRule.antiPattern: Do not keep two filter rows just because both already exist.

copyAndToneRules: Use short editorial framing, not hype.
Explain what the work proved, not just what was made.
Let outcomes and mechanisms do the persuasion.
likelyRuntimeFiles: related CSS Modules directly tied to the work index
antiPatterns: Rebuilding /work as a new information architecture.
Introducing a new taxonomy system in the same pass.
Promoting every sub-project equally because it has content.
Turning studio content into the primary trust layer.
Using additional motion, counters, or UI chrome to manufacture importance.
successCriteria: The page feels calmer within one screen of scanning.
A first-time visitor can identify the top proof items immediately.
System-child work reads as connected proof rather than clutter.
Browsing feels simpler, not more configurable.
The page looks more curated and less dashboard-like.
